title: Final style-lab selection reported NetworkError after persisting
summary: The final POST was written successfully, but --wait shut down the HTTP server before the browser finished consuming its response.
details: The server log and style_lab/selections.json proved the owner's const-default selection had persisted despite the browser error.
LabState now records response completion only after the handler writes and flushes the acknowledgement.
The wait loop holds the server through response delivery and a short browser grace period.
The final acknowledgement tells the page to close; the default launcher uses an isolated Chrome app window whose process can be closed without touching the owner's normal browser session.
If no Chromium-family browser is available, the system-browser fallback attempts window.close but may be restricted by browser policy.

provenance.note: Owner reported the visible NetworkError and requested final-tab closure.

provenance.reference: style-lab server log and persisted selections
provenance.note: POST returned server-side 200 and persisted before the wait thread terminated serving.

evidence.claim: A real localhost POST receives parseable complete JSON before response_finished_at is signaled.

questions: Why did the style lab report NetworkError even though the choice was saved?
How does the style lab close without killing normal browser tabs?

last_verified.method: Reproduced from logs and guarded with a live ThreadingHTTPServer/urllib acknowledgement test.
